threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the ‘Overground’ instead of just ‘the train’ and look confused when locals refer to ‘the bus’ with a number.
Heard a guy on the 196 bus raving about how the DJ at Electric Avenue used to mix just like his dad. You could feel the pride in his voice.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a guy playing the accordion while juggling flaming torches at Hermannplatz. That's Neukölln in a nutshell.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a café where customers pay based on how they feel that day. The honor system really brings out the best in people.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their ability to navigate the BART delays while still clutching their burrito. Tourists just look lost.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full Santa suit debating with a pigeon on Powell Street. Pigeon won, flew off with an abandoned french fry. Classic Muni.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just about the parks and plazas, but it’s really about the tiny, hidden bars where you hear real Catalan gossip over cheap vermut.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still taking photos of the Casa Ramos and asking locals for directions to Park Güell like it’s off the beaten path.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You’re still using that satnav to get to the local shops instead of just following the sound of the ice cream van.
Caught a night bus from Brixton and watched a guy offer to trade his pizza for someone’s spare change. The pizza was cold, but he was serious.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was in Neukölln when the guy in front of me at the Kebab stand started arguing passionately with the vendor about the best kind of sauce, and I realized it was a normal Tuesday.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find Döner stands open at 4 AM, serving perfectly crispy potatoes as a side—nothing else hits post-party cravings quite like it.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone's local when they instinctively check BART delays before planning a taco run, like it’s a spiritual rite.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus that stopped right at the turn for a taco truck. Half the riders hopped off for a quick bite. Talk about multitasking in a city that never stops!

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all locals drink beer in bars; most of us sip on vermut at home, especially on Sundays. There's a whole ritual we're missing out on.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their bewilderment at a local 'siesta' when everything shuts down in the afternoon—especially restaurants.
